channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: SEGA UNIVERSE initiative announced, spotlighting legacy IP with anniversary projects
item.content:encoded:
Sakura Wars, NiGHTS, and more celebrating major anniversaries.

SEGA has announced SEGA UNIVERSE, an initiative that shines a spotlight on legacy intellectual properties under the concept “NO OLD, STAY GOLD,” and introduces various projects celebrating their anniversaries. Details about these projects were not disclosed.

Here is an overview of the initiative, via its official website:

Introduction

Common sense? We decided to crash straight into it. We seriously chased the question, “Can’t this be more interesting?” And carved out landscapes no one had ever seen before.

Rough. Almost reckless. Sometimes spinning our wheels. But that’s exactly why it was real. Even when people laughed at the challenge, it shone brighter than anything else.

Time has passed. With changing eras, some things were lost. Still, there is an impulse that never changed. A glare that never faded.

Not to turn that recklessness into nostalgia. Not to say, “Those were the good old days.”

That frenzy is still alive somewhere, even now. Never rusted. Never dulled.

So let’s do it again. Next time, even bolder.

About

Games and characters born in the past.

Transcending generations and borders, they continue to live on in the same universe—even today.

Beyond games, expanding into film, music, fashion, and even further forms of entertainment.

While changing form, the experience is constantly updated.

Everything created here remains in someone’s memory and is passed on to the next generation.

2026 Selected

This article introduces the history of SEGA’s game titles and characters as they celebrate anniversaries.

The Companions who have brought color to the history of SEGA.

  1. Fantasy Zone (40th anniversary) – Launched as an arcade game. A flagship SEGA shooting game featuring innovative systems such as free-directional scrolling and a shop-based power-up system. Players control the warrior “Opa-Opa” and embark on a journey to protect the peace of Fantasy Zone by thwarting the conspiracy of an organization building a massive fortress. A shocking truth awaits at the end of the game…
  2. Out Run (40th anniversary) – Began operation as an arcade game. Later ported to numerous home consoles such as the SEGA Mark III, this is the original driving game. Surrender yourself to the music and speed through in a sports car—the exhilarating feeling of enjoying a premium drive is the core appeal of this title. In 2025, a film adaptation directed by Michael Bay, known for the Transformers series, was announced, bringing renewed attention to the title.
  3. Streets of Rage (35th anniversary) – The Streets of Rage series is widely popular as a belt-scrolling action game. The first title, Streets of Rage: Furious Iron Fist, was released on the SEGA Genesis. Depicts young fighters taking on a powerful syndicate using only their fists. The cooperative two-player mode—rare at the time—is one of its key appeals. The rhythmic soundtrack by Yuzo Koshiro received high acclaim, with a CD release as well.
  4. Rent A Hero (35th anniversary) – An action RPG where the protagonist rents a suit with superhuman powers and takes on various incidents as a hero protecting the city. Its quirky premise—where justice is powered by rented equipment—and unique humor throughout the missions are key charms. Gained popularity through word of mouth and was remade in 2000 as Rent A Hero No.1 for Dreamcast. The theme song by Hironobu Kageyama also became popular, and the title remains a cult classic.
  5. Guardian Heroes (30th anniversary) – An action RPG set in a world of swords and magic, where five adventurers who obtain the “Legendary Sword” fight alongside a mysterious undead hero against a kingdom.Features both a cooperative story mode and a VS mode for up to six players, making it enjoyable for groups.Its unique three-line battle system combines the exhilaration of action games with the strategy of fighting games.
  6. NiGHTS (30th anniversary) – A flight action game developed by Sonic Team. Children lost in a dream world set out on an adventure with NiGHTS to protect the beautiful dream realm “Nightopia.” The unique floating sensation of freely flying through a mysterious dream world is a defining feature. The limited “Christmas NiGHTS” release also gained attention. Its A-Life (Artificial Life) system allows inhabitants of the dream world to behave like living creatures, later evolving into the “Chao” in the Sonic Adventure series.
  7. Dynamite Deka (30th anniversary) – The industry’s first full-polygon action game. The Dynamite Cops storm a skyscraper occupied by terrorists to rescue the president’s daughter! Its over-the-top action—using everything from firearms to mops and grandfather clocks as weapons—drew attention, and even the commercial song became a hit when ported to SEGA Saturn. Even today, it stands out as a symbol of SEGA’s bold and unconventional style.
  8. Sakura Wars (30th anniversary) – A landmark dramatic adventure game set in a fictional Taisho era where steam technology has advanced. Its beautifully crafted animated sequences captivated many players. It won the CESA Awards (now Japan Game Awards) in 1996 and became the best-selling SEGA Saturn original title at the time. Its popularity expanded beyond games into anime, stage plays, drama CDs, novels, and manga, driven by its compelling characters and theme song “Geki! Teikoku Kagekidan.”
  9. SGGG (25th anniversary) – Released in 2001 for Dreamcast, SGGG is a simulation RPG set in a near-future SEGA. The protagonist, Taro Sega, joins the company and aims to reclaim dominance in the gaming industry. Its bold self-parody of SEGA’s own history gained attention, alongside an emotional story. It humorously yet realistically depicts the struggles of the game industry, including budget shortages and tight deadlines.

Watch the project concept movie below.

SEGA UNIVERSE Concept Movie



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/sega-universe-initiative-announced-spotlighting-legacy-ip-with-anniversary-projects
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Witch the Showdown launches in 2027 for PS5, Xbox Series, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

A fast-paced, high-difficulty parry-action deckbuilder.

Fast-paced, high-difficulty parry-action deckbuilder Witch the Showdown will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam in 2027, publisher Perp Games and developer CitalesGames announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Perp Games:

About

Precision is everything in Witch the Showdown, the new parry-action deckbuilder from Citales Games and Perp Games. This single-player near-future fantasy battler rewards mastery and perfect timing, all available in the Steam demo that’s available to play now.

Key Features

  • Unique Combat System – Where magic, machines, and metal collide, every encounter is a boss fight. Fast-paced action combines with strategic deckbuilding as you master the art of perfectly timed parries to fuel your mana and crush your powerful rivals. In this tournament, victory is the only half of the story.
  • Master the Cards – Designed for fans of intense action and competitive play, there is room for all ability levels. From Rookie through to Master, Witch the Showdown offers the option of five difficulties. Learn the ropes by taking on Duel Mode, allowing you jump straight into the action whilst you master the cards, their abilities, and finesse flawless parries with pre-built decks. Take on the toughest challenges as an experienced player or enjoy the unique combat system and story at your own pace.
  • Roguelike Rivalries – Forge your own path through to the championship in the roguelike Tournament Mode where you will face a roster of relentless rivals. Hand-pick your deck to match your playstyle, earn credits to unlock new cards and relics upon each victory, and fight your way to the top. With a fresh challenge waiting in every run, forge a deck as unique as the witch who wields it.
  • Modern Anime Aesthetic – Featuring a unique art-style rooted in contemporary anime, get to know Luze through her dedicated Manga in the demo. The full version, launching in 2027 across PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC will feature additional playable characters, rivals, and cards.
  • ❮
  • ❯

Steam Demo

  • Take on the Tutorial playing as Witch Luze. Learn the cards, master the moves, and tackle your tutor head-on in a high-stakes battle of skill and magic where you’ll experience the unique combat system first-hand
  • Ready to take on tougher opponents? Kick off Dual Mode. From Rookie through to Master, Witch the Showdown’s demo offers five difficulty levels. Jump straight into the action while you master the cards, their abilities, and finesse flawless parries with pre-built decks
  • Feeling confident? Ready yourself for the roguelike Tournament Mode. This slice of the story will see you face relentless rivals. Once you’ve mastered the cards, you can hand-pick your deck to match your playstyle. Victorious battles will allow you to earn credits and unlock new cards and relics to fight your way to the top of the championship
  • Get to know Witch Luze through her dedicated Manga in the demo. Witch the Showdown features a unique art style rooted in contemporary anime, bringing each character’s story to life

Witch the Showdown will be available for PC via Steam.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Gameplay Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/witch-the-showdown-launches-in-2027-for-ps5-xbox-series-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Toei Games announces first three published titles – KILLA, HINO, and DEBUG NEPHEMEE
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Games by talented creators from Japan and abroad.

Following the announcement of its establishment last week, Toei Games has announced the first three games it plans to publish—KILLA from Black Tangerine, HINO from UnGloomStudio, and DEBUG NEPHEMEE from Nephemee Studio.

“‘Story’ to Toei does not mean plotline,” the company said in a press release. “It is the summation of captivating characters and a world view that overwhelms the audience. Anything that has a strong and distinctive presence, falls within our definition of ‘story.’ Please enjoy this lineup of completely new and original works, brought to you by Toei in collaboration with talented creators from Japan and abroad.”

Here is an overview of each game, via Toei Games:

KILLA

  • Platform: PC (Steam)
  • Developer: Black Tangerine
  • Release Date: 2026

KILLA is a 3D adventure mystery with multiple endings. Valhalla, our protagonist, seeks the killer of her mentor, a mentor she treasures like family, venturing to an unknown island. Discover the culprit on this enigmatic island and embark on a journey of revenge.

Set in a nightmarish, dark, and fantastical puppet show world, KILLA is a mystery adventure. The player must use their “resonance” ability to delve into the suspects’ memories and piece together fragments of dreams to weave together the truth.

Who is the “La” who killed her mentor? The curtain rises now on this beautifully cruel stage

HINO

  • Platform: PC (Steam)
  • Developer: UnGloomStudio
  • Release Date: TBD

Join Hino and the whimsical Monimoni Skeleton as they traverse a shadow-cloaked world in this atmospheric 2D dark fantasy adventure.

Illustrated in ballpoint pen by the artist Yatara, the world of Hino comes to life as a game. In a shadow-drenched realm overrun by eerie monsters, the red-ribboned Hino and the quirky white Monimoni Skeleton set out on a journey in search of a safe haven. It is in your hands to guide them to that place of peace.

Through lands crawling with monsters and traps, you will uncover a single truth behind the creation of this dark world. When that truth is revealed, your choices will decide which of the many endings their journey will reach.

DEBUG NEPHEMEE

  • Platform: PC (Steam)
  • Developer: Nephemee Studio
  • Release Date: TBD

DEBUG NEPHEMEE is a top-down 2D adventure game set in a place called “Nepherum.”

Here, the world is infested with bugs who inhabit creatures called Nephemees.

Through a unique battle system based on each Nephemees values and philosophies, you will attack and hack opponents to find traces of their personalities and memories.

Encounter a colorful cast of characters and get to know them deeply, as you embark on a journey to “Debug” the world.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/toei-games-announces-first-three-published-titles-killa-hino-and-debug-nephemee
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: SUNSOFT announces HARD EDGE real-time tactical card battler remake Hard Edge: War Zone for PC
item.content:encoded:
SUNSOFT has announced Hard Edge: War Zone, a four-versus-four real-time tactical card battler remake of the 1998-released PlayStation action adventure game Hard Edge (known as T.R.A.G. in North America). It will launch for PC via Steam in 2026 with English, French, Italian, German, Spanish, Japanese, Korean, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese language options. A demo will be available as part of Steam Next Fest: June 2026 Edition from June 15 to 22.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page and official website:

About

HARD EDGE is back as a four-versus-four real-time tactical card battler! Combine strategic deck-building with simultaneous team actions. Every turn, draw cards to shoot, throw, or defend. Sacrifice cards to move in a high-stakes tactical “WAR ZONE.”

Read the Field, Shoot Your Cards!

Inheriting the gritty sci-fi world of the 1998 classic HARD EDGE, this title boldly reimagines the gameplay as a real-time tactical card battler. Engage in intense four-versus-four team-based combat with up to eight players!

Game Overview

Set in the hard-boiled cyberpunk world of the original HARD EDGE, this is an all-new tactical experience that blends the tension of a shootout with the strategic depth of a card game. Form a four-man squad and utilize your hand to annihilate the enemy team. Your tactics and teamwork will be put to the test in a war zone where turn-based deliberation meets real-time action.

Game System: Real-Time Tactical Card Battle

Combat progresses in turns. At the start of each turn, draw cards and choose your next move. The defining feature is that “all players on the same team act simultaneously.” Sync your timing with allies to suppress enemies with a barrage of fire, or have one player act as a decoy while the rest launch a surprise flank. Coordinated execution is the key to victory.

The Core of Strategy: Deck Building and Mid-Match Growth

Deck building—the heart of any card game—is vital here. Before the match, construct a unique deck tailored to your character’s personality and your personal playstyle. Furthermore, as characters level up during the match, you can add new cards to your deck or enhance existing ones. Victory requires both careful preparation and the ability to adapt your deck to the ever-changing battlefield.

Dominate the War Zone With Diverse Cards

Your deck contains various weapons, equipment, and support items. Choose the best card for the situation:

  • Ranged (Assault Rifles / Handguns / Sniper Rifles) – Powerful ranged attacks. You can snipe enemies in a straight line, but cover like walls will block your shot. Positioning for a clear line of sight is crucial.
  • Throwing (Grenades, etc.) – Deals area-of-effect damage. Perfect for flushing out enemies behind walls or wiping out clustered foes.
  • Combat (Knives, etc.) – High-risk, high-reward melee attacks that deal devastating damage if you can get close enough.
  • Defense (Armor, etc.) – Reduces incoming damage. An essential card for those serving as the team’s shield on the front lines.
  • Support and Tactics -Infinite tactical possibilities with “Medical Kits” to heal allies, “Extended Mags” to boost ammo, and “Deployable Cover” to create safety anywhere.
  • Movement – Positioning is key. Movement does not require a specific card; instead, you can discard ANY card from your hand to move. However, that card is lost. Will you discard your best attack card to escape danger, or stand your ground to save it for later? This “Ultimate Dilemma” defines the strategy of every step.
  • ❮
  • ❯

Two Tactical Game Modes

Adapt your strategy to the objective and lead your team to glory:

  • Capture the Flag – Compete for “Disks” containing vital data. The team with the most Disks when time runs out wins. Efficiency, route planning, and role distribution are more important than simple elimination.
  • Team Deathmatch – A pure, high-octane battle for dominance. The team with the most kills wins. Your combat skills, coordination, and card timing will decide your fate.

Story: The Prequel—“The Sakuraville Occupation”

Six months before the events of the original game… The “Sakuraville” research facility, owned by Machinery Gear, is suddenly seized by an unidentified armed group. Among the hostages is the high-profile scientist, Dr. Kevin. To resolve the crisis and rescue the doctor, authorities covertly deploy an elite special forces unit into the building. Among the members are Alex and Michelle—two individuals destined to be swept up in a harsh fate. Inside the sealed facility, heavy armaments and deadly traps await. Amidst clashing agendas, the desperate rescue operation begins now!

Build your deck, coordinate with allies, and survive the WAR ZONE!

Characters

  • Alex Barrat – AD 2046—an elite special operations agent deployed to the MG Corp. building siege. A consummate professional who prioritizes speed and efficiency above all, operating without emotion. All records are classified—her only calling card is an unidentified, trusted firearm.
  • Michelle Stevenson – An operative in the same unit as Alex. Excels in close-quarters combat with a high-frequency blade, backed by unmatched hand-to-hand skill within the ranks. Her strikes—fluid, yet relentless—shatter enemy defenses, leaving blue wings to cut across the battlefield.
  • Rachel Howard – A girl who holds the key to research buried in the shadows of a powerful corporation—her presence almost otherworldly. Though swept up in brutal conflict, she rises with unwavering resolve, a single light of hope in the WAR ZONE. Her innocent gaze stirs the humanity long forgotten by the operatives on the battlefield.
  • Burns Byford – A hardened man who infiltrated the MG building alone in search of a missing woman. Driven by a detective’s instincts and raw strength, he fights on with stubborn resolve to bring back what he’s lost. Behind his easygoing demeanor lies top-tier skill—he’ll break the chain of corruption with his own fists.
  • Kevin Howard – A former researcher in the bio-weapon development division, now imprisoned within MG Corp’s classified sector. Haunted by the realization that the intelligence he created has become a spark capable of destabilizing the world. Consumed by guilt and despair, he endures in isolation—driven to put an end to the tragedy he set in motion.
  • Cassandra Dupont – One of the masterminds behind the siege. She lived undercover as an office worker at MG Corp—her true nature, a cold beauty driven by a lust for control. Wielding brainwashing techniques and shapeshifting at will, she manipulates humans as mere “parts” to serve her desires.
  • Gasshu Heiworse – The mercenary leader behind the building’s takeover. His body fully converted into a cyborg, he crushes intruders with overwhelming firepower and defense. Unleashing a storm of destruction—ked by his built-in weapon, the “Gash Beam”—this berserker reduces all opposition to ash.
  • Brennum Arkley – One of the mercenaries occupying the building. Equipped with extendable bio-blades in both arms, capable of slicing through metal like paper. Employing tactics such as restraint and transformation, this ruthless hound never lets a captured target escape.

Watch some footage (without sound) from the official website below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Website Background Footage (No Sound)



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/sunsoft-announces-hard-edge-real-time-tactical-card-battler-remake-hard-edge-war-zone-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pixel art combo-packed character action game Knuckle Paradise announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

From ScourgeBringer developer Flying Oak Games.

Publisher ARTE France and ScourgeBringer developer Flying Oak Games have announced Knuckle Paradise a pixel art “non-stop character action game” inspired by classic Hong Kong action comedies. It will be available for PC via Steam. A release date was not announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Oh no! Jane has been abducted and forced into an underground fighting tournament! Punch your way with stylish combos and your sidekick… battle-chicken?! Freely explore an abandoned amusement park filled with drama and absurd situations in this non-stop character action game inspired by classic Hong Kong action comedies.

Spectacular Action, Non-Stop Drama, and… A Chicken?!

From the developers of ScourgeBringer and NeuroVoider comes Knuckle Paradise—a precise and juicy hand-drawn spectacle fighter! Inspired by classic Hong Kong action comedies and character action games (you know Dante and Bayo, right?): Knuckle Paradise combat is fast, it’s furious, it’s combo-based, and it’s so aerial you’ll send Jane—and Jane’s enemies—flying!

Play as Jane, a retired boxer who finds herself stranded on an abandoned amusement park island! Has she been abducted?! It’s worse than that: she’s been forced into an underground martial arts tournament taking place within the park!

It won’t take long for Jane to team up with Cocotte, a warrior princess chicken. Together, the duo will battle their way through the mysteries of the island, encountering and defeating powerful opponents along the way.

Use Cocotte’s special abilities to overpower your opponents in unexpected ways. Combat is satisfyingly punchy and immediately fun… while remaining accessible for many skill levels.

Freely Explore the Park and Have a Combo-Packed Adventure!

Are you a completionist who wants to explore, do side quests, and meet new characters? We’ve got loads of that! Find the hidden martial art magazines and unlock new skills for Cocotte while looking for protein bars to increase Jane’s maximum health. Infiltrate a night club with your stealth skills, participate in car chases, get lost in the mirror house…

Do you want to stick to brawling and not get distracted? Go for it! The park is full of hand-animated combatants who are all cruising for a bruising, plus you’re sure to run into some real trophy-seeking challengers in their big, open, boss-room-like areas. You’ll also meet a crew of endearing and quirky characters while beating your way through this story-rich, chicken-forward, deliciously dynamic character action adventure.

Watch the announcement teaser trailer below. Visit the official website here.

Announce Teaser Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/pixel-art-combo-packed-character-action-game-knuckle-paradise-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Perfect Tides: Station to Station for Switch launches May 14
item.content:encoded:
• Switch

Narrative adventure game currently available for PC.

Narrative adventure game Perfect Tides: Station to Station will launch for Switch on May 14, developer Three Bees announced.

Perfect Tides: Station to Station first launched for PC via Steam on January 22.

“I wanted to make sure that players have a smooth and seamless experience playing Perfect Tides: Station to Station on Switch, so we worked with Clickpulp to get the Switch controls just right,” said Three Bees founder Meredith Gran in a press release. “They’ve done a great job, and Mara feels right at home on the Nintendo Switch.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Three Bees:

Meredith Gran, creator of the hit webcomic Octopus Pie and the award winning Perfect Tides, fills each chapter of Perfect Tides: Station to Station with unforgettable characters and authentic life. Join fledgling writer Mara Whitefish as she begins her college years in the big city, where her competing desires to become a successful writer and be loved by others propels her into a series of tumultuous experiences and encounters that shape who she becomes as a person.

Navigate Mara’s newfound independence to balance education, relationships, and personal development as she struggles to find her footing as a young adult. Talk with new friends, lovers, and mentors to deepen relationships, and use the city’s mass transit system to explore more than 60 environments.

Keep track of names in Mara’s early 2000s’ brick-style cell phone, and collect important books and reading assignments to broaden her knowledge of new topics to discuss up with everyone she encounters. Each experience, assignment, and conversation present new opportunities for Mara to grow into the writer she dreams of becoming—or languish in mediocre obscurity.

Watch a new trailer below.

Switch Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/perfect-tides-station-to-station-for-switch-launches-may-14
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Echo Generation 2 details characters
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Xbox Series

Meet Jack, Bulder, Annata Z, and more.

Cococucumber has released a new trailer for cyberpunk turn-based RPG Echo Generation 2 introducing the game’s cast.

Here is an overview of the game and each character, via Cococucumber:

About

Echo Generation 2 follows Jack’s journey, introducing new characters such as Annata Z, Noliva, Strix, Sister M, and Bulder through interconnected chapters and changing perspectives. The turn-based deckbuilder features more than 150 cards, enabling players to wreak havoc with devastating combos, from protective shields to perfectly timed summons.

Characters

  • Jack – Echo Generation 2‘s main protagonist and the driving force behind the narrative. A former special ops agent turned family man, he is pulled back into conflict after a catastrophic breach tears at reality. As the world fractures, Jack must protect his family from escalating mutations and encroaching cosmic horrors from other dimensions.
  • Bulder – A mischievous creature from another world, cast onto Earth through the breach. Driven by a shared desire to return home, Bulder joins Jack on a perilous mission deep within the FST. As they uncover hidden experiments, they are drawn into a web of secrets where Dr. Lee, an old acquaintance, plays a dangerous game of trust.
  • Annata Z – A wandering exile who must piece together her lost memories on a stark industrial frontier. As fragments of her memories surface, they reveal a trail of loss and suffering. Fueled by grief and resolve, her journey becomes a relentless pursuit of vengeance for her daughter and for the suffering of her people.
  • Noliva – A charismatic bounty hunter who thrives in the chaos of the Neon District alongside her loyal companion, Strix. When echoes of her hidden past emerge, she is forced to confront reality, and the crime boss who raised her. The truth he holds could reshape everything she thought she knew, and the future of the galaxies.
  • Sister M – A defiant psychic teenager determined to escape the FST Research Center at any cost. As she searches for her missing friends, she uncovers disturbing truths buried within the facility. What she discovers reveals not only the institute’s purpose, but her role in the center of it all. Sister M was first introduced during the game’s demo.
  • Strix – Rescued from a deadly storm and has been inseparable from Noliva ever since. Raised together in the mean streets of Sectoris under the influence of a powerful crime lord, the duo forged an unbreakable bond. Now they take on dangerous bounties across a neon-lit city divided by rival factions battling for control.

Echo Generation 2 is due out for Xbox Series and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store on May 27. It will also be available via Game Pass.

Watch the trailer below.

Characters Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/echo-generation-2-details-characters
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
